title: AI porn startup sues Apple over App Store takedowns, claims $500,000 in lost revenue
snippet: AI startup Ex-Human is suing Apple for removing its apps, Botify AI and Photify AI, from the App Store. The company claims that the wrongful takedown resulted in &nbsp;$500,000 in lost revenue.&nbsp;

title: OpenAI, Anthropic contractor Mercor targeted in major security breach — what data was stolen, who carried out the hack?
snippet: Founded in 2023 and regarded as one of Silicon Valley's hottest start-ups, Mercor, valued at $10 billion, works with AI companies, including OpenAI and Anthropic, to help train models by contracting specialists such as doctors, scientists, lawyers, etc across various markets, including India.

title: Trump administration appeals ruling that blocked Pentagon action against Anthropic over AI dispute
snippet: Trump administration appeals ruling that blocked Pentagon action against Anthropic over AI dispute

title: Is X down? Thousands of users report issues with news feed in US: What we know
snippet: Elon Musk's platform X is reportedly facing another outage, preventing users from accessing feeds. As per users, issues include login failures and app crashes, marking the third disruption.&nbsp;

title: Realme 16 with iPhone Air like camera module and 7,000mAh battery launched in India, price starts at  ₹31,999
snippet: Realme has launched the Realme 16 in India with a 120Hz AMOLED display, 7,000mAh battery, and MediaTek Dimensity 6400 Turbo processor. The phone is at  ₹31,999 and it will be available from April 5&nbsp;&nbsp;

title: Vivo V70 FE with 200MP camera and 7,000mAh battery launched in India, price starts at  ₹37,999
snippet: Vivo has launched the V70 FE in India with a 1.5K AMOLED display, MediaTek Dimensity 7360 Turbo processor, and IP68/IP69 ratings. The phone starts at a price of  ₹37,999 and will be available for pre-order today&nbsp;

title: What is OpenAI’s ‘Spud’? Greg Brockman teases new ChatGPT model built on years of research
snippet: OpenAI's Greg Brockman has announced that the company's upcoming AI model ‘Spud’ is a result of two years of research. The new model will serve as a foundational base model for future ChatGPT iterations

title: Redmi Note 15 SE 5G with 6.77-inch AMOLED display and 5,800 mAH battery launched in India, price starts at…
snippet: Xiaomi has launched the budget-friendly Redmi Note 15 SE 5G with a 120Hz AMOLED display, Snapdragon 6 Gen 3 processor, and military-grade durability. The phone starts at a price of  ₹19,999 and will be available from April 7&nbsp;

title: ChatGPT and Anthropic could soon route extremist AI users to a new third-party tool: Report
snippet: A new tool being developed in New Zealand aims to direct individuals exhibiting violent extremist tendencies on ChatGPT to deradicalization support. The tool is being created by a company called ThroughLine.

title: Meta warns iPhone users of Italian spyware campaign disguised as WhatsApp: Here's what you need to know
snippet: WhatsApp has alerted around 200 users in Italy about a malicious version of the app containing spyware developed by SIO. Victims were tricked into downloading the fake app through social engineering techniques.

title: Big shakeup at OpenAI: COO Brad Lightcap shifts out of role; Fidji Simo to take medical leave
snippet: OpenAI faces major leadership changes as Brad Lightcap moves roles and Fidji Simo goes on health leave. Meanwhile, the company prepares for a possible IPO, expands revenue efforts like ads, and relies on executives to maintain continuity, growth, and progress during a pivotal period.

title: Elon Musk asks SpaceX IPO banks to buy Grok AI subscriptions, NYT reports
snippet: Some banks ​have agreed to spend tens of millions of dollars a ​year on the chatbot and have begun integrating it into their IT systems, the ​report said.

title: China moves to regulate digital humans, bans addictive services for children
snippet: The draft regulations would ​also ban the use of other people's personal information to create digital humans without ​consent, or using virtual humans to bypass identity verification systems, reflecting Beijing's efforts to maintain control in the face of advances in artificial intelligence.

title: SpaceX delays next Starship test launch by a month, Musk says
snippet: Musk posted on social media platform ‌X that ⁠the ⁠next flight of Starship's V3 vehicle was four ​to six weeks away, or in the first two ​weeks of May. Earlier, he said the first flight would take place in ​April.

title: Karnataka: Pralhad Joshi fears taxes on EVs will derail India’s emission targets
snippet: Union Minister Pralhad Joshi criticized Karnataka's decision to withdraw road tax exemptions for electric vehicles, warning it could increase India's crude import bill and hinder climate goals. title: Are stricter rules holding back fintech startups? QED Investors' Nigel Morris weighs in
snippet: India's fintech sector faces near-term challenges due to increased regulatory scrutiny, impacting early-stage startups' ability to scale.  This tightening, coupled with global economic uncertainties, has led to portfolio companies adjusting operations and tightening underwriting standards.

title: AI needs ‘better marketing’, says Sam Altman after buying media platform TBPN
snippet: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman is acquiring popular podcast The Best Possible Node (TBPN) to improve AI's public image, calling it a poorly marketed technology. Altman praised TBPN's ability to explain complex AI concepts accessibly and without hype. The podcast will retain editorial independence, reporting

title: Turkey launches investigation into Google over advertising, billing practices
snippet: The ‌investigation ​aims ​to determine ⁠whether Google's billing practices ​and commercial practices ​related to its online advertising services to advertisers ‌and advertising agency clients ​violate ​Turkish ⁠law, the board said in a statement.
